questions.question: Which of the following is the PRIMARY purpose of implementing a defense-in-depth strategy?
questions.option_a: To satisfy complex regulatory compliance checklists
questions.option_b: To ensure that if one security control fails, others protect the asset
questions.option_c: To minimize the annual cost of purchasing firewall equipment
questions.option_d: To encrypt all files on hard drives automatically
questions.explanation: Defense-in-depth implements layered, redundant controls (preventive, detective, corrective) to protect an asset. If a single control is compromised, subsequent layers prevent a complete compromise.

questions.question: Which of the following controls is the BEST example of a preventive control?
questions.option_a: Reviewing system database access logs weekly
questions.option_b: Installing a biometric lock on the server room door
questions.option_c: Restoring virtual machines from offline backup files
questions.option_d: Configuring email alerts for failed login attempts
questions.explanation: A biometric lock physically blocks unauthorized users from entry, making it a preventive control. Log reviews and alerts are detective, while backup restores are corrective.

questions.question: Which of the following controls is the BEST example of a detective control?
questions.option_a: Restricting database column access via role permissions
questions.option_b: Reviewing audit logs for unauthorized configuration modifications
questions.option_c: Running automatic software virus quarantine engines
questions.option_d: Encrypting customer password files in databases
questions.explanation: Reviewing audit logs detects violations or modifications after they have occurred. Permissions and encryption are preventive, while quarantine engines are corrective/preventive.

questions.question: An organization implements a secondary firewall because the primary firewall cannot support encryption rules. The secondary firewall is considered a:
questions.option_a: Preventive Control.
questions.option_b: Compensating Control.
questions.option_c: Administrative Control.
questions.option_d: Corrective Control.
questions.explanation: A compensating control is an alternative measure implemented when a primary control is not feasible or fails to meet the required security standard.

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important factor when designing a security awareness program?
questions.option_a: Using the same training slides for all employee roles
questions.option_b: Tailoring the training content to specific target audiences
questions.option_c: Minimizing the length of training sessions to under 5 minutes
questions.option_d: Focusing training purely on complex cryptography math
questions.explanation: Awareness training is most effective when customized for target audiences (e.g., developers need secure coding training, executives need spear-phishing alerts, general staff need basic password safety).

questions.question: The CISO wants to ensure that developers are writing secure code. Which of the following should be integrated into the SDLC?
questions.option_a: Annual compliance audits after software releases
questions.option_b: Static and dynamic application security testing (SAST and DAST)
questions.option_c: External database backup verification checks
questions.option_d: Restricting code editor folder access to local Admins
questions.explanation: SAST (reviewing code patterns) and DAST (testing running applications) are secure coding checks that locate flaws early in the SDLC, enabling developers to remediate before release.

questions.question: Which of the following access control models restricts privileges based on attributes of the user, resource, and environment?
questions.option_a: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
questions.option_b: Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)
questions.option_c: Mandatory Access Control (MAC)
questions.option_d: Discretionary Access Control (DAC)
questions.explanation: ABAC evaluates attributes (user department, resource classification, environmental factors like time or location) to make access decisions, providing extreme flexibility.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of a Change Control Board (CCB)?
questions.option_a: To write system patch code directly for administrators
questions.option_b: To evaluate, schedule, and approve system changes to minimize business disruption
questions.option_c: To audit database entries for regulatory compliance
questions.option_d: To manage the security awareness program materials
questions.explanation: The CCB reviews requested changes to assess operational risk, coordinate resource schedules, and ensure rollback plans exist, preventing unexpected outages.

questions.question: Which cryptographic method provides non-repudiation in electronic transactions?
questions.option_a: Symmetric AES encryption
questions.option_b: Digital signatures using public key cryptography
questions.option_c: Hashing the transaction logs with SHA-256
questions.option_d: Salting passwords in database files
questions.explanation: Non-repudiation prevents a sender from denying they sent a message. It is achieved via digital signatures, where the sender encrypts a hash using their unique private key.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary risk of using symmetric encryption for global data exchanges?
questions.option_a: High computational overhead compared to asymmetric math
questions.option_b: Secure distribution of the shared secret key to all parties
questions.option_c: Lack of support for standard security algorithms
questions.option_d: Slow data encryption speed for large files
questions.explanation: Symmetric encryption uses the same key for encrypting and decrypting. If the key must be shared globally, securely distributing and protecting the key is the primary challenge.

questions.question: An organization wants to centralize log collection and correlate events across servers. The CISO should recommend:
questions.option_a: a local Intrusion Detection System (IDS).
questions.option_b: a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system.
questions.option_c: an Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) agent.
questions.option_d: a Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB).
questions.explanation: SIEM systems aggregate log files from multiple hosts, correlate events in real time, and generate alerts, providing a unified security monitoring console.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of network segmentation in corporate networks?
questions.option_a: Increasing network bandwidth speed for public sites
questions.option_b: Restricting lateral movement of attackers if a breach occurs
questions.option_c: Ensuring database backup compliance
questions.option_d: Simplifying external audit inspections
questions.explanation: By dividing the network into isolated subnets, an attacker who compromises a single endpoint cannot easily access critical databases or server networks, limiting breach scope.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary responsibility of a Certificate Authority (CA) in a PKI framework?
questions.option_a: Encrypting local server hard drives
questions.option_b: Verifying identities and issuing digital certificates
questions.option_c: Filtering external network port scans
questions.option_d: Writing security awareness training guides
questions.explanation: The CA is a trusted entity that validates the identity of certificate requestors and signs digital certificates to bind those identities to public keys.

questions.question: Which of the following is the BEST way to mitigate the risk of Shadow IT?
questions.option_a: Enacting severe disciplinary actions for any software download
questions.option_b: Implementing Cloud Access Security Brokers (CASBs) and educating users
questions.option_c: Shutting down the company Internet access completely
questions.option_d: Permitting all software downloads without reviews
questions.explanation: Shadow IT is often used because corporate tools don't meet user needs. Monitoring via CASB provides visibility, while educating users aligns them with safe procurement channels.

questions.question: Which of the following concepts refers to the security state where a user's privileges are validated continuously before accessing any resource?
questions.option_a: Defense-in-depth
questions.option_b: Zero Trust Architecture
questions.option_c: Symmetric cryptography
questions.option_d: Role-Based Access Control
questions.explanation: Zero Trust operates on the principle 'never trust, always verify,' requiring continuous authentication and validation of users and devices for all resource requests.

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important factor when reviewing patch management performance?
questions.option_a: The total volume of patches released by vendors
questions.option_b: The time elapsed between patch release and system installation
questions.option_c: The amount of server RAM saved after updates
questions.option_d: The cost of downloading patch files from vendor sites
questions.explanation: The critical metric is the 'exposure window' (time between patch release/vulnerability public disclosure and installation). Minimizing this window reduces exploit probability.

questions.question: A CISO wants to ensure that database administrators cannot modify application source logs without detection. This is achieved via:
questions.option_a: Separation of Duties and write-once logs.
questions.option_b: Least Privilege permissions on columns.
questions.option_c: Encrypting all logs with symmetric keys.
questions.option_d: Using local admin passwords on database servers.
questions.explanation: Separation of Duties prevents a single user (even an administrator) from performing conflicting tasks, and sending logs to write-once media prevents audit tampering.

questions.question: The primary objective of implementing Data Loss Prevention (DLP) software is to:
questions.option_a: accelerate backup restore speeds.
questions.option_b: detect and block unauthorized transfers of sensitive data.
questions.option_c: compress database transaction tables.
questions.option_d: prevent database column deletion errors.
questions.explanation: DLP inspects data in use, in transit, and at rest to identify confidential patterns (e.g., credit card numbers) and block unauthorized leaks.

questions.question: What is the primary risk of using outdated cryptography algorithms like WEP or DES?
questions.option_a: High hardware costs for key processing
questions.option_b: Vulnerabilities to rapid mathematical decryption attacks
questions.option_c: Lack of technical vendor support
questions.option_d: Slow network transfer rates
questions.explanation: Outdated algorithms contain known cryptographic weaknesses and can be cracked in minutes by modern computing systems, providing zero protection.

questions.question: Which of the following is the best way to verify control effectiveness?
questions.option_a: Copying control definitions from policy guidelines
questions.option_b: Performing regular independent testing and audits
questions.option_c: Having system owners self-certify compliance
questions.option_d: Increasing the budget of the security program
questions.explanation: Independent testing (such as audits, penetration tests, or vulnerability assessments) provides objective verification of control existence and operational effectiveness.

questions.question: A company requires developers to use multi-factor authentication to log in to coding environments. This control is:
questions.option_a: Administrative.
questions.option_b: Technical.
questions.option_c: Physical.
questions.option_d: Compensating.
questions.explanation: Multi-factor authentication is a software/technical mechanism implemented on authentication gateways, making it a technical control.

questions.question: Which of the following should be defined FIRST when establishing an identity management system?
questions.option_a: The type of biometric reader hardware to purchase
questions.option_b: The business requirements and access policies of the organization
questions.option_c: The database database schema for user profiles
questions.option_d: The password complexity guidelines for general staff
questions.explanation: All systems must be driven by business requirements. Access policies define *who* gets *what* access, which must be established before choosing technology or database schemas.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most effective security control against SQL injection exploits on public web applications?
questions.option_a: Daily network vulnerability sweeps
questions.option_b: Input validation and parameterized database queries
questions.option_c: Restricting web port access to internal IPs
questions.option_d: Using symmetric encryption on web directories
questions.explanation: SQL injection occurs when user input is executed as database command code. Input validation and parameterized queries treat input as variables, blocking code execution exploits.

questions.question: The primary role of the Change Control Board during emergency security patching is to:
questions.option_a: block emergency changes to prevent operational disruption.
questions.option_b: expedite review while ensuring minimal testing and rollbacks exist.
questions.option_c: write code patches for the database administrators.
questions.option_d: notify regulatory compliance officers of the change.
questions.explanation: Emergency changes cannot follow slow approval timelines, but the CCB must expedite the process while ensuring testing is performed and a back-out plan exists.

questions.question: What is the primary objective of a post-implementation review of a newly installed security control?
questions.option_a: To confirm the control was purchased under budget
questions.option_b: To verify the control is operating correctly and achieving risk reduction goals
questions.option_c: To prepare training guides for the compliance audit team
questions.option_d: To write a press release detailing the new system
questions.explanation: A post-implementation review verifies that the control operates as designed, aligns with policies, and successfully mitigates the targeted risk exposure.

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST critical first step when establishing an incident management capability?
questions.option_a: Purchasing digital forensics write blocker hardware
questions.option_b: Defining what constitutes an incident (incident criteria)
questions.option_c: Setting up a 24/7 hotline with public relations
questions.option_d: Restoring database systems from backups
questions.explanation: Before you can respond to an incident, you must define the criteria for what is considered an event vs. a security incident, preventing alarm fatigue and guiding escalations.

questions.question: During which phase of the incident response lifecycle is log file aggregation and triage performed?
questions.option_a: Preparation Phase
questions.option_b: Identification (Triage) Phase
questions.option_c: Containment Phase
questions.option_d: Eradication Phase
questions.explanation: Log reviews and triage occur during the Identification phase, where the team detects, analyzes, and confirms that a security incident is actively occurring.

questions.question: An active data leak is detected on a database server. What is the priority incident response action?
questions.option_a: Perform a post-incident review (lessons learned)
questions.option_b: Contain the incident by isolating or taking the database offline
questions.option_c: Initiate forensic cloning of the database hard drive
questions.option_d: Notify the legal compliance team of a data breach
questions.explanation: Containment is the priority once an incident is identified. Isolating the system stops the active data leak immediately, minimizing damage.

questions.question: The phase of incident response that focuses on removing malware, disabling compromised accounts, and fixing vulnerabilities is:
questions.option_a: Containment Phase.
questions.option_b: Eradication Phase.
questions.option_c: Recovery Phase.
questions.option_d: Preparation Phase.
questions.explanation: Eradication focuses on removing the root cause of the incident from systems, paving the way for safe recovery operations.

questions.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of a Lessons Learned review meeting?
questions.option_a: To assign disciplinary actions to administrators
questions.option_b: To evaluate incident handling performance and improve future response capabilities
questions.option_c: To calculate the quantitative ALE of the threat event
questions.option_d: To write draft templates for compliance regulatory reports
questions.explanation: The Lessons Learned phase analyzes the incident timeline, identifies process bottlenecks or gaps, and updates policies/plans to improve future response.

questions.question: A forensic analyst must clone a compromised server drive. Which tool is critical to preserve the integrity of original evidence?
questions.option_a: A high-speed database query engine
questions.option_b: A hardware write-blocker device
questions.option_c: An encrypted USB storage card
questions.option_d: A local antivirus sweep scanner
questions.explanation: A write-blocker blocks write commands to the original media. This ensures that copying the data does not modify file timestamps or evidence headers, maintaining integrity.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of maintaining a strict 'Chain of Custody' document?
questions.option_a: To satisfy internal budget review committees
questions.option_b: To ensure digital evidence is legally admissible in court
questions.option_c: To track the salary history of forensic analysts
questions.option_d: To document server backup tape rotation schedules
questions.explanation: Chain of Custody documents every person who handled, transferred, or analyzed the evidence. Without this document, the evidence can be rejected as untrustworthy in court.

questions.question: An incident escalation matrix defines which of the following?
questions.option_a: The mathematical steps to restore database tables
questions.option_b: When and how to notify different management tiers or authorities based on severity
questions.option_c: The list of network ports blocked by firewalls
questions.option_d: The backup schedule frequency for host servers
questions.explanation: The escalation matrix maps incident severity levels (Low, Med, High, Critical) to corresponding communication flows and response timelines.

questions.question: Which disaster recovery site option is best suited when database downtime tolerance is less than 30 minutes?
questions.option_a: A cold site
questions.option_b: A warm site
questions.option_c: A hot site
questions.option_d: A reciprocal agreement site
questions.explanation: A hot site provides active data replication and ready hardware, enabling rapid failovers in minutes. Warm and cold sites require hours or days to configure.

questions.question: Which of the following BCP tests requires the highest level of coordination and introduces the most risk to business operations?
questions.option_a: A walkthrough review session
questions.option_b: A tabletop classroom exercise
questions.option_c: A parallel recovery test
questions.option_d: A full interruption cut-over test
questions.explanation: Full interruption tests shut down production systems to force the recovery site to take over, introducing high operational risks if recovery fails.

questions.question: A tabletop exercise is primarily designed to:
questions.option_a: restore server databases from tape backups physically.
questions.option_b: simulate incidents in a classroom environment to verify stakeholder roles and plans.
questions.option_c: test the electrical output of redundant power supplies
questions.option_d: measure network transit speeds between recovery sites
questions.explanation: Tabletop exercises are discussion-based reviews where key stakeholders sit around a table and walk through response checklists for hypothetical scenarios, validating plan logic.

questions.question: If an organization wants to test recovery site capabilities without shutting down any production databases, they should run a:
questions.option_a: Full Interruption test.
questions.option_b: Parallel test.
questions.option_c: Change control audit.
questions.option_d: Standard firewall scan.
questions.explanation: Parallel tests bring recovery systems online to process mock transactions in parallel with live production, verifying system capability without interrupting business operations.

questions.question: A CSIRT is primarily responsible for:
questions.option_a: updating corporate billing directories.
questions.option_b: handling and coordinating response to security incidents.
questions.option_c: auditing financial quarterly tax statements.
questions.option_d: testing the strength of physical fence wires.
questions.explanation: The CSIRT (Computer Security Incident Response Team) is the core operational group responsible for containing, analyzing, and resolving active security breaches.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of conducting a walkthrough test of a Disaster Recovery Plan?
questions.option_a: Restoring systems back online in parallel configurations
questions.option_b: Checking plans and contact directories for accuracy at low cost and risk
questions.option_c: Testing the battery capacity of local UPS backups
questions.option_d: Obtaining final SOC 2 compliance certificates
questions.explanation: Walkthrough tests involve stakeholders sitting down and reading through procedures, making it a very low-cost and low-risk way to find errors, outdated contacts, or gaps in plans.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most critical factor in determining the frequency of database backup operations?
questions.option_a: The maximum data loss tolerance (RPO)
questions.option_b: The target time to restore services (RTO)
questions.option_c: The file storage capacity of backup tape drives
questions.option_d: The annual rate of threat occurrence (ARO)
questions.explanation: RPO defines the maximum acceptable data loss. If the RPO is 8 hours, backups must occur at least every 8 hours. RTO dictates recovery speed, not backup frequency.

questions.question: A ransomware attack has compromised a corporate server, but the database logs are clean. The CISO should first:
questions.option_a: pay the threat actors to secure decryption key templates.
questions.option_b: activate the Incident Response Plan to contain the server.
questions.option_c: delete the database logs to hide the incident details.
questions.option_d: notify the media of a major corporate security breach.
questions.explanation: When an active threat is identified, the CISO must activate the IRP immediately. Paying ransom and notifying media occur in later phases, if at all.

questions.question: Which of the following recovery strategies is the most appropriate when the business has an MTD of 1 week and a limited budget?
questions.option_a: A fully synchronized active-active Hot Site
questions.option_b: A cold site with hardware procurement agreements
questions.option_c: A mobile recovery bus caravan
questions.option_d: A reciprocal agreement with another company
questions.explanation: An MTD of 1 week allows substantial time for recovery. A cold site is the most cost-effective solution, as it provides basic facilities while procurement agreements ensure hardware arrives on time.

questions.question: During a post-incident review, a CISO discovers that incident response was delayed because team members could not access the call list. What should be done?
questions.option_a: Discipline the team members for not memorizing the list
questions.option_b: Store the call list in multiple secure, offline, and online locations
questions.option_c: Delete the call list to avoid future access issues
questions.option_d: Assign call list management to external auditors
questions.explanation: Incident response plans and contacts must be accessible during a crisis, even if the primary network is offline. Storing copies in secure offline/alternative locations resolves this vulnerability.

questions.question: Which backup method copies only the files that have changed since the last full or incremental backup, resetting the archive bit?
questions.option_a: Differential Backup
questions.option_b: Incremental Backup
questions.option_c: Copy Backup
questions.option_d: Daily Backup
questions.explanation: Incremental backups copy changed files and reset the archive bit (flagging them as backed up). Differential backups copy changed files since the last full backup, without resetting the bit.

questions.question: Which of the following is the most important metrics to track during a disaster recovery test?
questions.option_a: The quantity of coffee consumed by the team
questions.option_b: Actual recovery times compared to target RTO and RPO metrics
questions.option_c: The amount of server RAM used during restoration
questions.option_d: The total page count of the disaster recovery plan
questions.explanation: The core objective of a DR test is to prove that systems can be restored within recovery thresholds (RTO, RPO). Tracking actual vs. target times measures success.
